LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF 1200 CALIFORNIA STREET CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST DEFENDANTS PAIGE STRUCTURAL GLASS CO. AND STAR GLASS CO., INC. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. -- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT HAS RECONSIDERED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND AMENDS ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN THAT THERE IS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO DEFENDANT'S BREACH OF IMPLIED WARRANTY BASED ON DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSION #41. HOWEVER, THE COURT STILL DENIES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BECAUSE THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE AMOUNT OF DAMAGES DUE TO THE BREACH. DEPT. OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS V. UI VIDEO STORES, INC. (1997) 55 CAL.APP.4TH 1084, 1097. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Kahn; Court Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-04-2011 Continued To Dec-14-11 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF 1200 CALIFORNIA STREET CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST DEFENDANTS PAIGE STRUCTURAL GLASS CO. AND STAR GLASS CO., INC.

APPEARANCES: Mohammad Walizadeh, Esq. 995-5802 & Theodore A. Kolb, Esq. 995-5098 for pltf & moving party; Adrienne E. Nordstrom, Esq. of Clapp Moroney Bellagamba Vucinich Beeman Scheley 925/734-0990 for deft & opposing party Paige Structural Glass Co. dba Paige Glass Company.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: THE COURT HAS RECONSIDERED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND AMENDS ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN THAT THERE IS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO DEFENDANT'S BREACH OF IMPLIED WARRANTY BASED ON DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSION #41. HOWEVER, THE COURT STILL DENIES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BECAUSE THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE AMOUNT OF DAMAGES DUE TO THE BREACH. DEPT. OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS V. UI VIDEO STORES, INC. (1997) 55 CAL.APP.4TH 1084, 1097. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
